title: ATF Task Force Overtime Reimbursement Agreement Renewal for Two Co-Designated Officers
summary: Five-year renewal of agreement with Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, and Firearms for overtime reimbursement covering two Syracuse Police Department task force officers co-designated with ATF. Reimbursements arrive timely. Council members commend SPD officers for community engagement with youth and procedural justice practices at incidents.
raw_content:
[00:26:59] stated, I have item number 21, which is
[00:27:01] a renewal of our agreement with the
[00:27:03] Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, and Firearms
[00:27:05] for overtime, uh reimbursement for um
[00:27:09] any cases that we do with ATF. We have
[00:27:11] two task force officers that are uh
[00:27:13] co-desated um with ATF. So any overtime
[00:27:16] that they incur during the course of
[00:27:18] their investigations with those folks
[00:27:19] and ATF, we get re uh reimbursement on
[00:27:22] that overtime. It's a 5-year agreement,
[00:27:23] and this is a renewal.
[00:27:26] Do they come to us timely? Those um
[00:27:28] reimbursements,
[00:27:29] >> they come timely. Yes, they do.
[00:27:31] >> Yep. Absolutely. Yep. Chief,
[00:27:33] >> is it a renewal or is it just because I
[00:27:35] have it as a new the letter was written
[00:27:37] as a new agreement?
[00:27:38] >> My understanding is it's a renewal, but
[00:27:40] I can confirm that.
[00:27:41] >> I'll look back on the index to see what
[00:27:43] it is and get in touch with you.
[00:27:44] >> Okay, sounds good. Thank you.
[00:27:45] >> Hey, chief. I just want to give a quick
[00:27:47] shout out to some of your staff. I
[00:27:50] actually two of them. Um, once I was at
[00:27:52] a game a couple weeks ago and a young
[00:27:55] kid wanted to see what the vest and
[00:27:57] stuff look like and you know the officer
[00:27:59] let him you know feel like the vest that
[00:28:01] was awesome and the kid was just
[00:28:03] thinking he was the officer and I
[00:28:04] thought that was great cuz that's great
[00:28:06] >> you know [snorts]
[00:28:07] you know with the children and the other
[00:28:09] thing was I was at an incident the other
[00:28:11] day and there was a
[00:28:14] >> was really ugly incident but I want to
[00:28:16] commend your staff because it was a
[00:28:17] little chaotic but at the end they were
[00:28:20] able to explain to the people in the
[00:28:22] community why they had to say what they
[00:28:25] said and why they, you know, it wasn't
[00:28:27] just like, you know, he made sure before
[00:28:29] they left that they explained to them
[00:28:30] the protocol and why they had to do it
[00:28:32] certain way and that it they weren't
[00:28:34] just being, you know, mean or so I'm
[00:28:37] saying to you is whatever you're doing
[00:28:38] to this new
[00:28:40] >> group that's coming through, they're
[00:28:42] definitely they're [snorts] hitting it
[00:28:43] out of those.
[00:28:43] >> Real really appreciate that. sounds a
[00:28:45] lot like the procedural justice that
[00:28:46] we're we're teaching to our folks and
[00:28:48] that's exactly what they should be
[00:28:49] doing, explanations and taking their
[00:28:51] time and and certainly the community
[00:28:53] engagement with the youth is is exactly
[00:28:55] what we want. So, it's great to hear.
[00:28:56] Thank you.
[00:28:57] >> All right. Thank you.
[00:28:59] >> Thanks, Chief.
[00:28:59] >> Thank you.
